title: Fix _git_lock class-level lock causing cross-test blocking
description:
## Context

The test suite has two tests that fail with worker crashes when run with pytest-xdist:

1. tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py::TestDependencyValidationAndRuntimeResilience::test_scheduler_skips_corrupt_ready_bead_with_missing_dependency
2. tests/test_recovery_flow.py::RecoveryBeadCreationTests::test_unrelated_failure_uses_corrective_not_recovery

## Root Cause

`RepositoryStorage._git_lock` is declared as a class-level attribute:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _git_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()
```

This means ALL `RepositoryStorage` instances share the same lock. In the test suite, `test_concurrent_writes_produce_no_index_lock_errors` in `test_scheduler_beads.py` creates 5 threads that compete for this lock. The test uses `t.join(timeout=10)` which exits even if threads are still alive. Those threads keep running while holding `_git_lock`.

When subsequent tests in the same pytest worker call `_git_commit_bead` (e.g., via `_record_missing_dependency_warning` → `_write_bead`), they block indefinitely waiting for `_git_lock` to be released. The `timeout_method = "thread"` in pytest-timeout cannot interrupt `threading.Lock.acquire()`, so the pytest-xdist worker crashes.

## Fix Required

In `src/agent_takt/storage.py`, change `_git_lock` from a class-level attribute to an instance-level attribute:

Change:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _git_lock: threading.Lock = threading.Lock()
    _auto_commit: bool = True
    
    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
        self.root = root.resolve()
        ...
```

To:
```python
class RepositoryStorage:
    _auto_commit: bool = True
    
    def __init__(self, root: Path) -> None:
        self._git_lock = threading.Lock()
        self.root = root.resolve()
        ...
```

This ensures each `RepositoryStorage` instance has its own lock, preventing cross-test contamination. In production there is typically one RepositoryStorage per repo root, so this does not change production behaviour.

## Expected Files to Modify

- `src/agent_takt/storage.py` — move `_git_lock` from class attribute to `__init__`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q` passes without worker crashes
- `uv run pytest tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py::TestDependencyValidationAndRuntimeResilience::test_scheduler_skips_corrupt_ready_bead_with_missing_dependency -v` passes
- `uv run pytest tests/test_recovery_flow.py::RecoveryBeadCreationTests::test_unrelated_failure_uses_corrective_not_recovery -v` passes
- No existing tests broken
